items.name: Greasy Pizza Box
items.hint: Greasy cardboard cannot be recycled – red bin!

items.name: Used Nappy
items.hint: Nappies always go in the red general waste bin

items.name: Plastic Bag
items.hint: Soft plastics go in the red bin – not yellow!

items.name: Takeaway Coffee Cup
items.hint: Takeaway cups have a plastic lining – not recyclable, red bin!

items.name: Polystyrene Container
items.hint: Polystyrene foam is not accepted in NZ kerbside recycling

items.name: Broken Glass
items.hint: Broken glass is dangerous – wrap & red bin it! (Not the blue glass bin)

items.name: Dog Poo Bag
items.hint: Bag it, bin it – general waste only

items.name: Dead Battery
items.hint: Batteries are hazardous – take to a Resource Recovery centre!

items.name: Chip Packet
items.hint: Foil & plastic wrappers go in the red bin

items.name: Dirty Noodle Container
items.hint: Dirty food containers cannot be recycled

items.name: Cling Wrap
items.hint: Cling wrap is a soft plastic – red bin

items.name: Disposable Cutlery
items.hint: Disposable cutlery is generally not recyclable

items.name: Bubble Wrap
items.hint: Bubble wrap is a soft plastic – cannot be recycled kerbside

items.name: Juice Carton
items.hint: Juice cartons (tetra pak) cannot be recycled kerbside – red rubbish bin!

items.name: Aerosol Can
items.hint: Aerosol cans are NOT accepted in the yellow recycling bin – red bin!

items.name: Broken Crockery
items.hint: Broken ceramics & crockery cannot be recycled – wrap safely & red bin

items.name: Broken Drinking Glass
items.hint: Drinking glasses go in the red bin – NOT the blue glass recycling bin!

items.name: Water Bottle
items.hint: Remove the lid first, then pop in the yellow bin!

items.name: Coke Bottle
items.hint: Plastic PET bottles – lid off, rinse, yellow bin

items.name: Milk Bottle
items.hint: HDPE milk bottles are recyclable – lid off, rinse, yellow bin

items.name: Beer Can
items.hint: Aluminium cans – rinse before recycling in the yellow bin

items.name: Tin Can
items.hint: Steel cans are recyclable – give them a rinse!

items.name: Shampoo Bottle
items.hint: Plastic bottles (grade 2) – lid off, rinse, yellow recycling!

items.name: L&P Bottle
items.hint: Kiwi classic – plastic bottle, lid off, yellow bin!

items.name: Milo Tin
items.hint: Steel tins are fully recyclable – Kiwi favourite!

items.name: Empty Paint Tin
items.hint: Completely empty, dry metal tins can go in yellow bin

items.name: Newspaper
items.hint: Paper & newspapers go in the yellow recycling bin

items.name: Large Cardboard Box
items.hint: Large cardboard must be cut down & flattened before recycling!

items.name: Printer Paper
items.hint: Clean paper is recyclable – yellow bin!

items.name: Old Magazine
items.hint: Magazines & glossy paper go in the yellow recycling bin

items.name: Weetbix Box
items.hint: Flatten the cardboard box and put in the yellow recycling!

items.name: Toilet Roll Tube
items.hint: Cardboard tubes are recyclable – yellow bin!

items.name: Junk Mail
items.hint: Paper junk mail goes in the yellow recycling bin

items.name: Clean Gift Wrap
items.hint: Non-foil gift wrap is paper – yellow recycling bin!

items.name: Egg Carton
items.hint: Clean & dry egg cartons are cardboard – flatten & yellow bin!

items.name: Old Paperback Book
items.hint: Books & paper go in the yellow recycling bin

items.name: Apple Core
items.hint: All food scraps go in the green organics bin

items.name: Banana Peel
items.hint: Fruit peels are great for the green organics bin

items.name: Vegetable Peels
items.hint: Veggie scraps are composted from the green bin

items.name: Egg Shells
items.hint: Egg shells are 100% compostable – green bin!

items.name: Fish Bones
items.hint: Meat, fish & bones all go in the green bin

items.name: Used Tea Bag
items.hint: Tea bags & coffee grounds are compostable

items.name: Garden Clippings
items.hint: Chop clippings smaller for easier composting

items.name: Stale Bread
items.hint: Old food goes in the green organics bin

items.name: Coffee Grounds
items.hint: Coffee grounds are excellent in the compost!

items.name: Half-eaten Weetbix
items.hint: Soggy cereal – food waste to the green bin

items.name: Avocado
items.hint: Avocado skin and pit are organic – green bin!

items.name: Meat Scraps
items.hint: Meat, bones & leftover food go in the green organics bin

items.name: Wine Bottle
items.hint: Glass wine bottles – rinse and recycle in the blue glass bin!

items.name: Pasta Sauce Jar
items.hint: Glass jars – remove lid, rinse out, blue glass bin!

items.name: Champagne Bottle
items.hint: Glass champagne bottles go in the blue glass recycling bin!

items.name: Honey Jar
items.hint: Glass honey jars – remove lid, rinse, blue glass bin!

items.name: Glass Beer Bottle
items.hint: Glass bottles belong in the blue glass recycling bin – not cans!

items.name: CFL Light Bulb
items.hint: Fluorescent & CFL bulbs contain mercury – Resource Recovery only!

items.name: Leftover Paint
items.hint: Partially-used paint is hazardous – take to Resource Recovery!

items.name: Old Medication
items.hint: Old pills & medication go to a pharmacy or Resource Recovery centre

items.name: Used Motor Oil
items.hint: Used oil is hazardous – Resource Recovery centre only!

items.name: Old Electronics
items.hint: E-waste contains hazardous materials – Resource Recovery!

items.name: Chemical Spray Bottle
items.hint: Household chemicals are hazardous – Resource Recovery centre!

items.name: Old Smoke Detector
items.hint: Smoke detectors contain radioactive material – Resource Recovery!

items.name: Car Battery
items.hint: Car batteries are highly hazardous – Resource Recovery centre only!

items.name: Flax (Harakeke) Leaves
items.hint: Flax fibres tangle compost machinery – NOT organics. Red bin (or specialist drop-off if your game adds it).

items.name: Kiwi Grass (Cabbage Tree / Tī Kōuka)
items.hint: Cabbage tree fibres don’t break down well and can tangle machinery – NOT organics. Red bin (or specialist drop-off if enabled).

items.name: Vacuum Cleaner Dust
items.hint: Vacuum dust is mixed fibres and grit – red rubbish bin.

items.name: Used Cat Litter
items.hint: Pet litter is not compostable in kerbside systems – red bin.

items.name: Cigarette Butts
items.hint: Cigarette filters contain plastic and toxins – red bin.

items.name: Chewing Gum
items.hint: Gum is synthetic – red bin only.

items.name: Wet Wipes
items.hint: Wet wipes contain plastic fibres – red bin.

items.name: Paper Towel
items.hint: Paper towels are not accepted in organics collections – red bin.

items.name: Used Tissue
items.hint: Used tissues are contaminated – red bin.

items.name: Shredded Paper
items.hint: Shredded paper is too small for sorting and often becomes litter – red bin.

items.name: Plastic Straw
items.hint: Small plastics fall through sorting lines – red bin.

items.name: Toothbrush
items.hint: Mixed plastics aren’t accepted kerbside – red bin.

items.name: Disposable Razor
items.hint: Razor heads are mixed materials – red bin.

items.name: Kitchen Sponge
items.hint: Sponges aren’t recyclable kerbside – red bin.

items.name: Medicine Blister Pack
items.hint: Plastic/foil blister packs are composite – red bin.

items.name: Used Bandage
items.hint: Medical/sanitary waste goes to red bin.

items.name: Broken Mirror
items.hint: Mirrors and window glass are not container glass – red bin.

items.name: Pyrex / Oven Glass
items.hint: Heat-treated glass doesn’t recycle with bottles/jars – red bin.

items.name: Window Glass Piece
items.hint: Window glass is not collected as container glass – red bin.

items.name: Plastic Lid
items.hint: Small lids fall through sorting machinery – red bin.

items.name: Metal Jar Lid
items.hint: Small metal lids are often excluded – red bin.

items.name: Foam Meat Tray
items.hint: Foam trays are polystyrene – red bin.

items.name: Thermal Receipt
items.hint: Thermal paper contaminates paper recycling – red bin.

items.name: Yoghurt Pottle
items.hint: Rigid plastic pottles – rinse and yellow bin.

items.name: Clean Takeaway Container
items.hint: If it’s clean rigid plastic (1,2,5), it can go in yellow.

items.name: Ice Cream Tub
items.hint: Clean rigid plastic tubs (1,2,5) go in yellow.

items.name: Laundry Detergent Bottle
items.hint: Rigid plastic bottles (1,2,5) – lid off, rinse, yellow bin.

items.name: Empty Cleaning Spray Bottle
items.hint: Empty, rinsed spray bottles (1,2,5) go in yellow.

items.name: Clean Aluminium Tray
items.hint: Clean metal trays can go in yellow as metal recycling.

items.name: Envelope (No Plastic Window)
items.hint: Plain paper envelopes are recyclable – yellow bin.

items.name: Old Phone Book
items.hint: Books/paper are recyclable – yellow bin.

items.name: Paper Shopping Bag
items.hint: Paper bags are recyclable – flatten first.

items.name: Clean Pizza Box
items.hint: If it’s clean with no food/grease, cardboard can go in yellow.

items.name: Plastic Egg Carton (Rigid)
items.hint: Rigid plastic cartons (1,2,5) can go in yellow if clean.

items.name: Orange Peel
items.hint: Citrus peels are fine in organics bins.

items.name: Onion Skin
items.hint: Onion skins are food scraps – green bin.

items.name: Loose Tea Leaves
items.hint: Loose tea leaves are compostable – green bin.

items.name: Fallen Leaves
items.hint: Leaves are garden waste – green bin.

items.name: Small Twigs
items.hint: Cut twigs smaller – garden waste to green bin.

items.name: Dead House Plant
items.hint: Remove the pot – plant material goes in organics.

items.name: Olive Jar
items.hint: Glass jars – lid off, rinse, blue glass bin.

items.name: Pickle Jar
items.hint: Glass jars – lid off, rinse, blue glass bin.

items.name: Jam Jar
items.hint: Glass jars – lid off, rinse, blue glass bin.

items.name: Fire Extinguisher
items.hint: Fire extinguishers are hazardous – Resource Recovery only.

items.name: Paint Thinner
items.hint: Solvents & thinners must go to Resource Recovery.

items.name: Fluorescent Tube
items.hint: Fluorescent tubes contain mercury – Resource Recovery only.

items.name: Small Gas Cylinder
items.hint: Gas cylinders are hazardous – Resource Recovery only.

alternatives.note: If a separate glass stream exists, putting glass in recycling is allowed with reduced score.
Rubbish is always allowed but scores 0 unless the item was rubbish by default.

material_hint_overrides.hint: Glass bottles and jars go in the yellow recycling bin (no separate glass bin in this district).

alternatives.note: Rubbish is always allowed but scores 0 unless the item was rubbish by default.

material_hint_overrides.hint: Glass is collected separately, but the container is GREEN here (instead of the standard blue glass bin).

alternatives.note: Always allowed, but scores 0 unless default was rubbish.

material_hint_overrides.hint: Glass is excluded from kerbside recycling; preferred path is resource collection/drop-off. Red bin is allowed but not ideal.

alternatives.note: Allowed fallback path; reduced points vs drop-off/resource collection.

regions.region: Western Bay of Plenty District Council

notes.coverage: This list targets all 67 NZ territorial authorities (city councils, district councils, Auckland Council, Chatham Islands Council).
notes.about_inheritance: Game engine should resolve effective rules by applying district overrides on top of rule_set referenced by metadata.inheritsFromRuleSet.

confidence_values.sourced: Ruleset selection is based on a council-specific source already discussed in this chat.
confidence_values.assumed_shared_rules: Assigned to share a nearby/related ruleset for gameplay convenience (not independently verified here).
confidence_values.assumed: Defaulted to NZ standard ruleset as a placeholder until you supply/crawl council-specific rules.
